title: Causal Telemetry, Event-Loop Capacity, Scheduling, and Operations · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in operable privacy-aware crawler service and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Correlate run, policy, URL, attempt, redirect, response, parse, frontier, browser, audit, export, and recovery events without logging content secrets.
Define low-cardinality request, byte, queue, age, error, rejection, browser, report, and budget metrics with units and labels.
Measure event-loop delay, CPU, heap, streams, sockets, browser contexts, queue age, throughput, and origin fairness under representative fixtures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A debug TypeScript team handles case 7538 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile causal telemetry, bounded metrics, event-loop capacity, non-overlap schedules, and runbooks; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define startup, pause, incident, partial output, retention, deletion, correction, restart, rollback, and escalation procedures with owners.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Runbook Retention
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-runbook-retention-1vus82" that uses case 4480 to reconcile causal telemetry, bounded metrics, event-loop capacity, non-overlap schedules, and runbooks. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Runbook Retention.
